title: OLEIC ACID
description: Oleic acid is a monounsaturated omega-9 fatty acid commonly found in olive oil, nuts, and various seeds. It functions as an anti-inflammatory compound that modulates cellular signaling pathways including PI3K/AKT-Nrf2 and AMPK-mTOR, affecting lipid metabolism, insulin sensitivity, and oxidative stress. Oleic acid is used primarily for metabolic health, cardiovascular protection, and anti-inflammatory purposes.

research.title: Dietary oleic acid supplementation and blood inflammatory markers: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
research.summary: Meta-analysis of 31 RCTs involving 1,634 subjects found that oleic acid supplementation significantly reduced C-reactive protein (CRP) levels, indicating anti-inflammatory benefits, though it did not significantly affect other inflammatory markers like TNF or IL-6.

research.title: Oleic acid abomasal infusion limits lipolysis and improves insulin sensitivity in adipose tissue from periparturient dairy cows
research.summary: Oleic acid infusion reduced adipose tissue lipolysis and improved insulin sensitivity in dairy cows during the periparturient period, suggesting beneficial effects on metabolic regulation and energy balance.

research.title: Oleic acid ameliorates amyloidosis in cellular and mouse models of Alzheimer's disease
research.summary: Oleic acid supplementation reduced amyloid-beta levels and BACE enzyme activity in Alzheimer's disease models, decreased amyloid plaques, and increased non-amyloidogenic APP processing, suggesting neuroprotective effects.

research.title: Free Linoleic Acid and Oleic Acid Reduce Fat Digestion and Absorption In Vivo as Potent Pancreatic Lipase Inhibitors Derived from Sesame Meal
research.summary: Oleic acid demonstrated potent pancreatic lipase inhibitory activity with an IC50 of 11.7 µg/mL and significantly suppressed fat digestion and absorption in rats, suggesting potential for weight management applications.

research.title: Dietary oleic acid intake increases the proportion of type 1 and 2X muscle fibers in mice
research.summary: Dietary oleic acid supplementation increased the proportion of slow-twitch type 1 and oxidative type 2X muscle fibers in mice, suggesting potential benefits for endurance and metabolic muscle characteristics.

research.title: Nitro-oleic acid regulates growth factor-induced differentiation of bone marrow-derived macrophages
research.summary: Nitro-oleic acid, a metabolite of oleic acid, was found to regulate macrophage differentiation and modulate immune responses, demonstrating anti-inflammatory properties in bone marrow-derived cells.

research.title: Hesperetin ameliorates hepatic oxidative stress and inflammation via the PI3K/AKT-Nrf2-ARE pathway in oleic acid-induced HepG2 cells and a rat model of high-fat diet-induced NAFLD
research.summary: Study used oleic acid to induce hepatic steatosis models, demonstrating its role in fatty liver research and establishing that interventions targeting oxidative stress pathways can ameliorate oleic acid-induced liver damage.

research.title: Dehydroepiandrosterone alleviates oleic acid-induced lipid metabolism disorders through activation of AMPK-mTOR signal pathway in primary chicken hepatocytes
research.summary: Research utilized oleic acid to model lipid metabolism disorders in hepatocytes, demonstrating that interventions activating AMPK-mTOR pathways can reverse oleic acid-induced metabolic disturbances.

sentiment.summary: Oleic acid receives minimal direct discussion in user forums as a standalone supplement, appearing primarily in research contexts and as a component of other supplements. When mentioned, it's generally recognized for anti-inflammatory properties but lacks substantial user experience data for assessment.

effects.label: Inflammation Reduction:
effects.description: Meta-analysis of 31 RCTs showed significant CRP reduction with 10% decrease per 10g fiber increase

effects.label: Fat Metabolism:
effects.description: Demonstrated pancreatic lipase inhibition comparable to pharmaceutical options, reducing fat absorption

effects.label: Research Model Use:
effects.description: Frequently used in studies to induce metabolic conditions, indicating well-understood cellular mechanisms

effects.label: Dose-Dependent Benefits:
effects.description: Effects appear to scale with intake, though optimal dosing ranges vary by application

dosage.label: Research Doses:
dosage.description: Studies used varying amounts from 0.5-1.0 mM oleic acid in cellular models

dosage.label: Dietary Sources:
dosage.description: Naturally obtained through Mediterranean diet components like olive oil, nuts, and avocados

dosage.label: Supplement Forms:
dosage.description: When supplemented directly, often combined with other fatty acids rather than isolated

dosage.label: Food-Based Approach:
dosage.description: Most evidence supports obtaining oleic acid through whole food sources rather than isolated supplements

sideEffects.label: Cellular Stress:
sideEffects.description: At high concentrations, oleic acid can induce hepatic steatosis and lipid accumulation in cell models

sideEffects.label: Metabolic Disruption:
sideEffects.description: Excessive intake may contribute to lipid metabolism disorders in susceptible individuals

sideEffects.label: Research Safety:
sideEffects.description: Human trials generally report good tolerability when consumed as part of balanced dietary fats

sideEffects.label: Limited Standalone Data:
sideEffects.description: Few reports of side effects from isolated oleic acid supplementation in humans

availability.label: Food Sources:
availability.description: Readily available through olive oil (55-85% oleic acid), avocados, nuts (especially macadamias and almonds)

availability.label: Supplement Form:
availability.description: Not commonly sold as isolated oleic acid supplement; typically consumed through dietary sources or mixed fatty acid products

availability.label: Research Grade:
availability.description: Available as research chemical for laboratory use but not marketed for human supplementation
